hypothesis: If an LLM agent is augmented with external scientific tools and closed-loop feedback via diffusion-based structure generation and learned property evaluation, then it will achieve higher success rates (within ±0.25 eV/atom of target formation energies) and higher V.U.N. scores than tool-free LLMs or diffusion-only baselines because tool-informed reasoning and memory guide proposals toward chemically plausible, stable, and novel regions of materials space.
